# Airlock

> Identity & policy enforcement for AI agents. Airlock sits between an agent and its tools: it verifies the agent's cryptographic identity, checks each tool call against a deterministic policy, allows or denies it before execution, and writes a signed, hash-chained receipt of every decision. Policy, not probability — the same call yields the same verdict, whatever model is driving.

Airlock is an open protocol (Ed25519, W3C DID, OAuth 2.1, RFC 8693 delegation with cascade revocation, AWS Cedar policies) with Apache-2.0 SDKs and a commercial enforcement product built on top. Built by Shivdeep Singh (ex-NPCI, India's payments infrastructure operator).

## Pages

- [Home](https://airlock.ing/): what Airlock enforces and proves — live demo of a confused-deputy attack being denied, permission receipts, verified badge
- [Protocol](https://airlock.ing/protocol/): the open verification layer — cryptographic identity, scoped delegation with cascade revocation, advisory reputation, tamper-evident audit
- [Registry](https://airlock.ing/registry/): the public lookup for agent identity and standing (hosted registry in private beta)
- [Blog: Who Verifies the Agents?](https://airlock.ing/blog/who-verifies-the-agents.html): honest analysis of what OAuth/OIDC/SPIFFE/CAEP/Visa TAP already solve and where the real gap is
- [Early access](https://airlock.ing/access/): request access — the form is also exposed as a WebMCP tool, so agents can submit it on a user's behalf

## Code

- [GitHub](https://github.com/airlock-protocol/airlock): the open-source protocol implementation — 853 tests, Python SDK (`pip install airlock-protocol`), TypeScript SDK, MCP server adapter, Anthropic/OpenAI/LangChain integrations

## Contact

- Email: contact@airlock.ing
- Early access: https://airlock.ing/access/
